behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real filesystem watch recursion and event delivery from OS. MOCK-MISS/MOCK-HIDE: in-process watcher fakes can miss real recursive-watch gaps. REAL-INFRA: yes (real FS events). LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no. Rejected higher levels because behavior text has no AI/ML step or multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon against a real temp directory tree, create `root/sub/a.txt`, assert exactly one transformed output appears in output dir for that subdirectory file.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: Boundary token is 'deduplication logic' with path+time-window rule, which is local decision logic. MOCK-MISS: no required cross-module handoff in the behavior text. REAL-INFRA: no (can use fake clock). Rejected Integration/System because behavior text does not require OS/network/subprocess interaction; rejected Agentic/Workflow because no AI/ML dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Call dedup component twice with same canonical file path under fake clock; verify first request accepted, second rejected inside window, third accepted after window expiry.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly cites OS duplicate events, so real failure appears at OS watcher boundary. REAL-INFRA: yes. MOCK-HIDE: mocked watcher often emits idealized single events and can hide duplicate-event bugs. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI/ML or multi-agent sequence in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: With daemon running on real watched dir, perform a write pattern known to trigger multiple FS events (create+append+rename), then assert pipeline ran once for that file.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: Failure depends on real OS directory lifecycle events and watcher re-subscription behavior. REAL-INFRA: yes. MOCK-HIDE: fake FS/watchers can skip edge semantics around inode replacement/recreate. Rejected higher levels since behavior text has no model decision or chained agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon, delete watched directory, recreate it at same path, place new file, assert it is processed without restarting daemon.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Ordering is tied to real file creation timestamps and arrival conditions; OS timestamp resolution and event timing can cause real failures not seen in pure mocks. REAL-INFRA: likely yes to catch true boundary behavior. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no AI/ML output dependence. Integration was considered but behavior text ('arrive at the same time', 'creation timestamps') points to OS-mediated timing/metadata boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Create multiple files in a real watched directory with controlled creation times close together, then verify processed order matches filesystem creation timestamp order.

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: This is a real I/O race between writer and watcher; exact failure depends on OS event timing and file-lock/flush semantics. REAL-INFRA: yes. MOCK-HIDE: fake writes usually complete atomically and hide partial-write pickup bugs. Rejected Agentic/Workflow due no AI/ML decision path in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Open a file for incremental writes in watched dir, trigger arrival event during write, ensure daemon defers processing until writer closes/flushed completion, then processes once.
